records.ety: [L. panicula a tuft on plants, dim. of panus the thread wound upon the bobbin in a shuttle; cf. Gr. <?/, <?/; prob. akin to E. pane: cf. F. panicule. See 2d Pane.]

defs: A pyramidal form of inflorescence, in which the cluster is loosely branched below and gradually simpler toward the end.
